message:
fix(server): accurate drain statistics and immediate force-close responsiveness

ConnectionManager.begin_drain now snapshots the active connection count
at drain initiation and stores it atomically, so drain_with_stats
reports the correct total even when connections close between
begin_drain and the first poll of the drain future. Previously,
connections that closed in that window were silently lost from the
drained count.

The drain loop now polls a force-close phase waiter alongside the
connection-close and deadline notifications using poll_fn instead of
Select, so an external trigger_immediate call is noticed within one
poll cycle rather than waiting for the next connection close or timer
tick.

ShutdownSignal.trigger_immediate now transitions to ForceClosing
(instead of directly to Stopped), records a zero-length drain deadline
at the current time, and preserves an existing drain_start timestamp.
This gives drain_with_stats the metadata it needs to produce correct
drained vs force_closed counts. The caller must still call mark_stopped
to reach the terminal phase. Double-triggering on an already-Stopped
signal is a no-op.

Tests added:
- drain_with_stats correctly counts connections closed before the
  future is first polled
- drain_with_stats returns synchronously when trigger_immediate fires
  concurrently, with correct force_closed count
- trigger_immediate records drain metadata without a prior begin_drain
- trigger_immediate is idempotent on an already-stopped signal
- Existing wait_for_phase test updated for ForceClosing semantics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
